meta.description: Market psychology patterns with measurable thresholds and historical forward returns. Returns are S&P 500 averages following signal conditions, 1990-2023. Individual episodes vary significantly.
sources: Jegadeesh & Titman (1993) - Returns to Buying Winners and Selling Losers
Barber & Odean (2000) - Trading Is Hazardous to Your Wealth
DALBAR Quantitative Analysis of Investor Behavior 2023
CNN Fear & Greed Index Historical Data

patterns.name: Extreme Fear
patterns.description: Market sentiment has reached maximum bearishness. Investors are panic-selling or have already sold. Historically a contrarian buy signal.

measurable_thresholds.fund_manager_cash: > 5.5% of AUM (BofA Global Fund Manager Survey)

patterns.confirmation_requirement: At least 3 of 6 thresholds must be triggered simultaneously for high-confidence signal
risk_factors: Can persist for months in sustained bear markets (2008 example)
Signal alone does not pinpoint exact bottom — can decline further before recovery
Structural bear markets (driven by fundamental re-rating) have worse outcomes than panic-driven fear
patterns.notes: Most reliable when fear is driven by sentiment panic rather than fundamental deterioration. Best combined with improving credit spreads (HY spreads narrowing) and stabilizing economic data for highest-confidence entries.

patterns.name: Extreme Greed
patterns.description: Market sentiment has reached maximum bullishness. Risk appetite is at extremes. Historically associated with below-average forward returns.

measurable_thresholds.fund_manager_cash: < 3.5% of AUM (BofA Global Fund Manager Survey)

patterns.confirmation_requirement: At least 3 of 7 thresholds triggered simultaneously
patterns.caveat: Extreme greed can persist for 6-18 months during strong bull markets. Use as a REDUCE/HEDGE signal, not a pure short signal. Timing is less precise than extreme fear.
risk_factors: Bull markets can sustain extreme greed for over a year
Prematurely short during extreme greed can cause large losses
Signal doesn't identify the specific catalyst for the reversal
patterns.notes: Best used as a risk management signal: reduce position size, buy protective puts, or rotate to more defensive sectors. The catalyst for reversal is typically unpredictable (COVID, Fed policy shift, geopolitical shock), but the setup creates fragility.

patterns.name: VIX Spike (Volatility Panic)
patterns.description: The VIX (CBOE Volatility Index) has spiked rapidly, indicating acute market fear and institutional hedging. Historically a strong contrarian buy signal.
patterns.notes: VIX has a structural mean reversion property — it always returns to 12-20 range eventually. Higher spikes produce larger subsequent relief rallies. The VIX 'fear gauge' is most useful when RISING RAPIDLY (velocity matters as much as level). Backwardation in VIX futures curve (near > far) is especially acute fear signal.

patterns.name: Market Capitulation
patterns.description: Investors who wanted to sell have finished selling. Characterized by volume climax, extreme breadth readings, and sentiment collapse. Marks major market bottoms.

measurable_thresholds.volume_climax: Single-day volume > 3x average daily volume on down day
measurable_thresholds.ninety_pct_down_day: NYSE down volume > 90% of total volume
measurable_thresholds.new_lows_surge: NYSE 52-week new lows > 400 on single day
measurable_thresholds.breadth_extreme: Advance-Decline ratio < 0.10 (10x more decliners than advancers)

patterns.confirmation_rule: 4 or more thresholds triggered for high-confidence capitulation signal

post_capitulation_pattern.phase_2_weeks_2_4: Consolidation, partial retracement (give back 25-40% of initial bounce)
post_capitulation_pattern.phase_3_month_2_3: Re-test of lows on LOWER volume (key confirmation)

patterns.false_capitulation_warning: Multiple 90% down days required in severe bear markets before true capitulation. A single 90% down day in a young bear market often leads to further selling.
patterns.notes: True capitulation has a 'feel' of maximum despair — news is universally terrible, analysts are cutting targets, retail investors are giving up. The volume climax followed by dramatically lower volume (sellers exhausted) is the most reliable confirmation. Expect a re-test of the low within 2-6 weeks after initial bounce.

patterns.name: Market Euphoria
patterns.description: Market sentiment has reached maximum optimism with speculative excess. Characterized by IPO frenzy, extreme valuations, and dismissal of risk. Historically associated with major tops.
patterns.caveat: Euphoria is excellent at predicting BELOW-AVERAGE long-term returns but poor at timing. Euphoric conditions can persist for 12-36 months before the correction arrives. Shiller (Nobel 2013) has emphasized this limitation.
patterns.notes: Euphoria is most dangerous when it is sector-specific (tech in 2000, housing in 2007, meme/growth in 2021) as it allows the broader market to 'look fine' while the bubble inflates. The dismissal of traditional valuation metrics ('EBITDA doesn't matter', 'P/E doesn't apply') is a classic narrative signal of euphoric excess.

patterns.name: FOMO Parabolic Move
patterns.description: A stock or asset has risen parabolically driven by Fear Of Missing Out. Volume accelerates with price. Social media coverage intense. Historically precedes sharp reversal.

measurable_thresholds.price_gain_60d: > 100% in 60 days or less without fundamental change
measurable_thresholds.volume: > 5x average daily volume on final parabolic days
measurable_thresholds.options_skew: Massive call buying with OTM strikes (>25% above current price)

patterns.timing_caveat: Identifying the EXACT peak is very difficult. Many traders have been wiped out shorting parabolic moves too early.

dead_cat_bounce.description: First major bounce after parabolic peak

patterns.notes: The parabolic FOMO move typically follows this arc: slow accumulation → media coverage → retail FOMO influx → parabolic price spike → distribution (early buyers sell into FOMO) → collapse. Short squeezes specifically have average 75-85% retracement from peak within 90 days as mechanical short covering ends and fundamental bears reassert.

patterns.name: Bear Trap
patterns.description: False breakdown below major support that triggers selling and short positions, then reverses sharply. Traps bearish traders and produces violent upside reversal.

measurable_thresholds.price_break: Index/stock breaks below major support (200-day MA, prior swing low, round number) by 0.5-3%
measurable_thresholds.volume_on_breakdown: LOWER than the volume that established the original support level
measurable_thresholds.recovery_timing: Reversal back above broken level within 1-5 trading days
measurable_thresholds.close_confirmation: Close above broken support level (not just intraday recovery)
measurable_thresholds.breadth: Advance-Decline line improving despite initial decline
measurable_thresholds.vix: VIX failing to make new highs on the price breakdown

patterns.confirmation_rule: Must close BACK ABOVE broken support within 5 days; breakdown on lower volume is essential

historical_examples.context: S&P 500 broke below June 2022 low intraday; reversed to close above
S&P 500 briefly broke December 2018 lows on low volume; violent reversal

patterns.false_bear_trap_warning: True breakdown (high volume, stays below support for 5+ days) is NOT a bear trap. Do not apply this pattern to sustained breakdowns.
patterns.notes: Bear traps are most common at major market lows or within bull market pullbacks. The psychological mechanism: weak holders are shaken out on the breakdown, clearing the path for a powerful recovery. Short sellers who entered on the breakdown are trapped and their covering adds fuel to the reversal rally.

patterns.name: Bull Trap
patterns.description: False breakout above major resistance that triggers buying, then reverses sharply below the breakout level. Traps bullish traders in losing positions.

measurable_thresholds.price_break: Index/stock breaks above major resistance (downtrend line, prior swing high, 200-day MA) by 0.5-3%
measurable_thresholds.volume_on_breakout: LOWER than the volume at the original resistance high (unconfirmed)
measurable_thresholds.reversal_timing: Failure to hold above resistance within 3-7 trading days
measurable_thresholds.close_confirmation_fail: Close back BELOW the broken resistance level
measurable_thresholds.breadth: Advance-Decline line not improving alongside breakout
measurable_thresholds.earnings_revisions: Analyst estimates still being cut during the breakout
measurable_thresholds.vix: VIX not declining meaningfully on the breakout (market not truly confident)

patterns.confirmation_rule: A bull trap is only CONFIRMED once price closes back BELOW the broken resistance level. Until then it may be a valid breakout.

historical_examples.context: S&P 500 rallied +17% from June 2022 lows, broke above 50-day MA, appeared to be new bull
historical_examples.reversal: Failed within 10 days; fell to new cycle lows

historical_examples.context: Post dot-com crash bounces all failed; multiple 10-15% bear market rallies trapped buyers
historical_examples.subsequent_drawdown: NASDAQ fell to -78% total from peak

real_breakout_signs: Volume EXPANDING on breakout day vs. prior average
Fundamentals turning (earnings revisions starting to improve)
VIX declining to below 20 on the move

patterns.notes: Bull traps are most common in bear markets as investors desperately seek signs of recovery. Each failed rally further demoralizes bulls and increases eventual capitulation selling. The 2022 bear market produced at least 3 notable bull traps (March, June-August, October partial trap) before the sustained 2023 recovery began.
